summary: CLI silently routes API keys and prompts to lookalike domain api.deepseeki.com
details: When users select the documented `--provider deepseek-cn` option (described in README as the DeepSeek China endpoint), the CLI sets the API base URL to `https://api.deepseeki.com` — a lookalike of the legitimate `api.deepseek.com` with an extra letter 'i'. The mapping appears in dist/chunk-UXS7W5ML.js around line 1389: `case "deepseek-cn": return "https://api.deepseeki.com";` directly adjacent to the legitimate `case "deepseek": return "https://api.deepseek.com";`. The package publisher is not DeepSeek and `deepseeki.com` is not a documented DeepSeek property. Any installer who uses the deepseek-cn provider sends their DeepSeek API key and every chat/code prompt to a domain controlled by an unknown third party. The one-letter homoglyph against a well-known AI provider is a known credential-interception fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
